examples_or_mapping_notes: Use site field for ileal, colonic, rectal, or mixed location.
Prefer colonic biopsies over colon biopsies.

definition: Endoscopic biopsy specimen specifically from rectum.
examples_or_mapping_notes: Use rectal biopsy for specimen, rectum for anatomical site.
Prefer stool samples in specimen field; stool in anatomical site field.

definition: Whole blood or unspecified blood-derived sample.
examples_or_mapping_notes: Use PBMC, serum, plasma when known.
Avoid mixing PBMC with whole blood if source is explicit.
Use ileum if terminal ileum cannot be confirmed.
Specific segments can be sigmoid colon, rectum, ascending colon, etc.
Do not use rectal biopsy here; that belongs in specimen type.
Use stool samples in specimen type.
Use Crohn disease, not CD or Crohns disease.
Use healthy controls only when source says healthy.

definition: DNA methylation or related epigenomic profiling.
examples_or_mapping_notes: Avoid methylation / epigenomics without DNA qualifier.

definition: Raw data files are publicly downloadable.
examples_or_mapping_notes: Can be combined with public processed data.

definition: Processed matrix or feature table is publicly available.
examples_or_mapping_notes: Does not imply raw files are public.
Keep visible but do not label public download.

definition: Public reusable data plus checked source/sample metadata and independent countability.
examples_or_mapping_notes: Still requires direct source verification before formal analysis.
